zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron Richard de Bertram於1135年出生在英國諾圖波藍德米特福德米特福德時，他的父親William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair56歲，母親Hawise Balliol33歲。他於1153年與Baroness Menebell Gisulf在英國諾圖波藍德波塔爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1911年住在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德莫普斯莫普斯 堂史普。他去世於1196年之後在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baron Richard de Bertram于1135年出生在英国諾圖波藍德米特福德米特福德时，他的父亲William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair56岁，母亲Hawise Balliol33岁。他于1153年与Baroness Menebell Gisulf在英国諾圖波藍德波塔爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1911年住在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德莫普斯莫普斯 堂史普。他去世于1196年之后在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bertram
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Beltrami
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Dino, Marco, Pio, Sal, Tullio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Beltrame, from an ancient Germanic name composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Bartram
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: variant of   Bertram  .

History: William Bartram, a Quaker, had a large farm near Darby, PA, when his eldest son, John, the first American botanist, was born in 1699. John conducted botanical experiments at his own farm in Kingsessing, PA, near Philadelphia.

variants.name: Bertman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Frieda, Wilhelm.
Americanized form of German Bertmann: altered form (by folk etymology) of Bertram .

variants.name: Bethke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Eldor, Ewald, Otto, Rudi, Wilhelmina.
North German: from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal names   Bertram   or   Berthold  . See also   Beth  .

variants.name: Bertrand
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Monique, Francois, Gilles, Raoul, Emile, Herve, Jacques, Jean-Marie, Odette.

French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Bertrand, a variant of Bertram , coined by folk etymology under the influence of the present participle endings -and, -ant.
History: This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Beltran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Pedro, Jorge, Mario, Ricardo, Francisco, Rafael.
Spanish (Beltrán), Catalan, and southern French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. See also Bertram .

variants.name: Beltramo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Beltramo, of ancient Germanic origin (see Bertram ).

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Battermann
meanings: German:
possibly an occupational name for a merchant or peddler, from Bartermann.
alternatively, perhaps from a short form of the personal name Bertram + Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bertran
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jorge, Eduardo, Enrique, Joaquin, Ricardo, Aleida, Ana, Ana Maria, Elena, Felipe, Jeronimo.  French Andre.
French, Occitan, and Catalan: variant of   Bertram   or   Bertrand  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Baron Richard de Bertram was born in 1135, in Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England, his father, William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair, was 56 and his mother, Hawise Balliol, was 33. He married Baroness Menebell Gisulf in 1153, in Bothal, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom in 1911. He died after 1196, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron Richard de Bertram est né en 1135, à Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Hawise Balliol, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Baroness Menebell Gisulf en 1153, à Bothal, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni en 1911. Il est décédé après 1196, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baron Richard de Bertram 1135 in Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair 56 und seine Mutter Hawise Balliol 33. Er hat 1153 in Bothal, Northumberland, England Baroness Menebell Gisulf geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1911 hat er in Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom gelebt. Er starb nach 1196 in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baron Richard de Bertram è nato il nel 1135 nel Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, England, il suo padre, William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair, aveva 56 anni e il suo madre, Hawise Balliol, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Baroness Menebell Gisulf nel 1153, nel Bothal, Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito nel 1911. Morì dopo il 1196, nel Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Bertramが1135年にイギリスノースウンバーランドミトフォードミトフォードで生まれた時，父William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fairは56歳，母Hawise Balliolは33歳であった。1153年にイギリスノースウンバーランドボサルでBaroness Menebell Gisulfと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1911年にはイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドモーペスモーペス トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1196年以後にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron Richard de Bertram(이)가 1135년 영국 노르스벌랜드 미트퍼드 미트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair(은)는 56세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise Balliol(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1153년 영국 노르스벌랜드 벗할에서 Baroness Menebell Gisulf(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1911년도에 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 모르페스 모르페스 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1196년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baron Richard de Bertram nasceu em 1135, em Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Hawise Balliol, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Baroness Menebell Gisulf em 1153, em Bothal, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1911. Ele faleceu após 1196, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron Richard de Bertram родился в 1135 г. в местности митфорд, митфорд, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Hawise Balliol, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Baroness Menebell Gisulf в 1153 г. в местности ботэл, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности морпет тоуншип, морпет, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в 1911 году. Он умер после 1196 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baron Richard de Bertram nació en 1135, en Mitford, Mitford, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, William Bertram 2nd Baron of Mitford, the fair, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Hawise Balliol, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Baroness Menebell Gisulf en 1153, en Bothal, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Morpeth Township, Morpeth, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1911. Murió después de 1196, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
